description: The following recommendations should be considered while configuring the IP addresses for primary and backup controllers: While configuring a primary-controller ip <ipv4-address>, it is recommended to use the IP address of a standalone controller or an IP address of a controller in the primary cluster. Similarly, while configuring a backup-controller ip <ipv4-address>, it is recommended to use the IP address of a standalone controller or an IP address of a controller in the backup cluster. Configuring both primary or backup controller IP addresses from the same cluster is not supported.

description: User-Based Tunneling (UBT) and DHCPv4/v6 snooping are mutually exclusive features in AOS-CX. When these two features are configured simultaneously, binding entries for the end clients will not be populated in the binding database. IP source-lockdown feature permits traffic from the clients to pass through the switch only if the client's MAC and IP addresses are present in the binding database. IP source lockdown can be enabled at both port and VLAN levels. The port-level configuration will be overridden by the VLAN-level configuration only when the port is a member of a single VLAN. If the port is a member of multiple VLANs, then the VLAN-level configuration for IP source lockdown will not be applied. Client IP tracker can track the IP address until the IP source lockdown is applied. Once the lockdown is in effect, the IP address will only be tracked if there is a valid entry in the binding table. However, if an IP address is already being tracked, it will remain active through probes, as probes utilize ARP packets, which are Layer 2 (L2) packets rather than Layer 3 (L3) packets.

description: Symptom: Intermittent MSTP/RPVST starvation messages observed coming from source mac address 00:00:00:00:00:00. Scenario: When 3rd party legacy devices send packets with source mac address 00:00:00:00:00:00 to CX devices intermittent BPDU starvation might be observed. description: Symptom: Packets near MTU limits in size may not be present in a port or tunnel/remote based mirror. Scenario: When a client configures a mirror where the destination interface has the same MTU size, mirrored packets that are close to max MTU are incorrectly dropped and not mirrored. This applies to remote/tunnel mirrors as well. Workaround: Configuring mirror destination ports as routing port and set the IP MTU size. For a VLAN interface, set the IP MTU on the VLAN SVI. IP MTU must be set to accommodate the ethernet overhead +tag. For the default MTU, 1500, the IP MTU would be 1518. For a tunneled scenario, the MTU must also account for the tunnel header.

This applies to remote/tunnel mirrors as well. Workaround: Configuring mirror destination ports as routing port and set the IP MTU size. For a VLAN interface, set the IP MTU on the VLAN SVI. IP MTU must be set to accommodate the ethernet overhead +tag. For the default MTU, 1500, the IP MTU would be 1518. For a tunneled scenario, the MTU must also account for the tunnel header.

description: Symptom: ops-switchd crash while collecting VXLAN diagdump or collecting support files all. Scenario: In some VXLAN scenarios with multiple LAGs and multiple overlay VLAN on those lags. After device reboot, the LAG interface could show incorrect VXLAN Programming. If the user attempts to remove VLANs, VNI-to-VXLAN configurations that belong to the affected LAGS, a ops-switchd crash might be observed. Workaround: Ensure LAG interfaces are up before configuring the VLANs for the LAG interface.

Workaround: Use the CLI for VLAN access configuration instead of SNMP.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: Intermittent MSTP/RPVST starvation messages observed coming from source mac address 00:00:00:00:00:00. Scenario: When 3rd party legacy devices send packets with source mac address 00:00:00:00:00:00 to CX devices intermittent BPDU starvation might be observed. Workaround: Configure ACL to drop invalid source mac address 00:00:00:00:00:00 in all switches where end devices are connected.

description: Symptom: ops-switchd crash while collecting VXLAN diagdump or collecting support files all. Scenario: In some VXLAN scenarios with multiple LAGs and multiple overlay VLAN on those lags. After device reboot, the LAG interface could show incorrect VXLAN Programming. If the user attempts to remove VLANs, VNI-to-VXLAN configurations that belong to the affected LAGS, a ops-switchd crash might be observed. Workaround: Ensure LAG interfaces are up before configuring the VLANs for the LAG interface.
